VIA FACSIMILE and U.S. MAIL
Mary Ann Mason
Deputy County Counsel
Contra Costa County Counsel's Office
651 Pine Srreet, gth Floor
Martinez, CA 94553
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-08-029
Dear Ms. Mason
Re:
This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of David Bowlby, an
Area Planning Commissioner for the County of Contra costa, regarding the conflict-of-
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 This letter should not be
construed as advice on any condttct that may have already taken place. (See Regulation
18329(bX8XA).) In addition, this letter is based on the facts presented. The Fair
Political Practices commission (the "Commission") does not act as a finder of fact when
it renders assisrance. (In re Oglesby (1975) I FppC Ops. 71.)
QUESTIONS
l) If the planning commissioner recuses himself on rhe record at the San Ramon
valley Regional Planning Commission meeting regarding the decision involving the tree
permit appeal, and he leaves the dias:
A) May he remain in the audience during the Commission's consideration of the
matters?
' The Political Reform Act is contained in Gnvernment code sections til000 through gl0l.l. All
statutory references are to the Government Code. unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair
Political Practices commission are contained in Sections Itil l0 througr, rsqqz of Title 2 of the califirrnia
Code of Regulations. All regulatory ret'erences are to Title 2. Divisioi 6 of the calitirrnia code of
Regulations. unless otherwise indicated.


File No. A-08-029
Page No. 2
B) May he make public comment on these matters as a resident of the planned
Unit Development District in the same way and from the same location where members
of the public can comment?
2) While the tree permit appeal is pending before the San Ramon Valley Regional
Planning Commission, can the Commissioner, aCting as a representative of the
homeowner, communicate with members of the goaid of Supervisors about the appeal?
3) Once the decision of the San Ramon Valley Regional Planning Commission on
the tree permit appeal is final, if the matter is then appealed to the noard-of Supervisors:
A) May the commissioner, acting as a representative of the homeowner,
communicate with planning Department staff about the appeal?
B) May the commissioner, acting as a representative of the homeowner
communicate with members of the Board of Supervisors about the appeal?
C) During public hearings before the Board, may the commissioner appear on the
homeowner's behalf?
D) During public hearings before the Board, may the commissioner comment on
the appeal as a member of the public who lives in the planned unit development
district?
4) If the Planning Department's decision on modification of the Final
Development Plan is appealed to the Board of Supervisors:
A) May the Commissioner, acting as a representative of the homeowner
communicate with Planning Department staff about the appeal?
B) May the Commissioner, acting as a representative of the homeowner,
communicate with members of the Board of Supervisors about the appeal?
C) During public hearings before the Board, may the Commissioner appear on the
homeowner's behalf?
- 
D) During public hearings before the Board, may the Commissioner comment on
the appeal as a member of the public who lives in the pianned unit development district?
CONCLUSIONS
1. Due to Commissioner Bowlby's conflict of interest in the tree permit decision
1! Uecause of his position as planning commissioner (a position enumerated in Section
87200), Mr. Bowlby must recuse himself on the record ai the planning commission
meeting and leave the room for the duration of the discussion and,/or vote on the item. He


File No. A-08-029
Page No. 3
cannot remain in the audience and may not comment during the commission,s
consideration of the matter because based on the facts provlded the exception in
18702.5(d)(3) does not apply in this situation.
2-4: Based on the facts you have presented, decisions and appeals before the San
lamon Valley Regional Planning Commission and the Board of Suplrvisors are
decisions before Commissioner Bowlby's own agency. Therefore, he is subject to the
broad prohibition which prevents him from communicating with any member, officer,
employee or consultant with his agency, which, accordingio your facts, is composed of
the various divisions ollh: counry's planning agency uniit, staff, including pranning
department staff. In addition, you donot pr"r.rt sufficient facts to support?hat the
Commissioner's personal interest is affecGd by the above decisions. i'he.efo.e, *e
conclude that he does not qualify for the exception under Regulation 1g702.4(b)and may
not comment on the appeals.
FACTS
You are seeking advice on beharf_of David Bowlby, an Area planning
commissioner for the county of Contra Costa. You wish to know whether ti. .rruy
communicate with planning department staff and the Board of Supervisor, ."garoing
ap-peals of a tree permit and modification of a Final Development plan for a residence in
a Planned Unit Development District. You also wish to know whether the Commissioner
may make public comments about these items before the planning commission and the
Board of Supervisors.
Govemment Code Section 65100 prcivides that each county has a planning agency.
with the powers necessary to carry out local planning functions and requires the county to
assign the functions of the planning agency within th-e agency as deemed appropriate. By
ordinance, the County of Contra Costa has established a planning agency consisting of
the following divisions: Board of supervisors; planning bommiJsiJr; eianning
Department; Board of Appeals; TnningAdministrator; and Area planning corimissions;
and has assigned responsibilities to each of these divisions. (Contra Cosla County
ordinance Code sections 26-2.202 ff.) The Planning Department acts as staff to all
9il'-t]ol' of the planning agency. (Contra Costa cointybrdinance code sections 26-
2.808 ff.)
A homeowner in a Planned Unit Development District wishes to demolish: (l) an
existing single family residence of approximateiy 8,000 square feet located on a 2zparcel
acre: (2) additional associated structures of approxim ately'6,342 sqllare feet lgarages,
apartment, theater' pool house, gorf house, staLles, and gazebo); and (3) other"
improvements including a parking area, tennis court anJa swimming poor.
The homeowner wishes to construct a larger home of approximately r g,000
square feet. along with other residential improveirents includin g a l,452rqru." foo,
garage, a 1,000 sqlrare foot second unit, and approximately 1,00b square f".t of n.*

Page No. 4
accessory structures. The homeowner applied to the Planning Department for a tree
permit and that permit was issued. ln conjunction with issuance of the tree permit, a
Mitigated Negative Declaration *at pr.pu.ed and approved by the zoni.rf,iaministrator
Several property owners in the Planned unit Development District have filed
comments with Planning Department to oppose the Mitigated Negative Declaration and
to assert that it is inadequate. opponents oi the project ,ioy upp.ol the issuance of the
tree permit, including approval of the Mitigated Negatiue Declaration, to the San Ramon
Valley Regional Planning Commission, thJ erea pt"anring Commission of which the
commissioner is a member. Either party may appeal that-planning commission,s
decision to the Board of Supervisor.. ttl, aniici^patea that the various appeals could be
heard between late March and July of this y"ur. i, addition to issuing the tree permit, the
Planning Department decided thaithe homlowner,, p.oj"", did not require a
modification of the Final Development Plan for ttre itannea Unit Deveiopr*n, District.
That decision is appealabre direcily to the Board or superuisors.
The commissioner owns a residence in the Planned unit Development District
that is approximately 2,900feet (over one-half mile) from the homeowner,s property.
The issuance of the tree permit, incruding approvar of the Mitigated Negative
Declaration, and the decision on the pinal rjevelopmenrpian are the actions necessary for
the homeo_wner's project to proceed. Concern has been "*p..rr"o by at least one
neighbor that approval of the homeowner's project would 
"uur" other properties in the
Planned Unit Development District to become targeted for the construction of
significantly larger homes.
In his private capacity, the Commissioner has a contract to provide development-
related services to the homeowner. The commissionei will receive more than $500 in
income from the homeowner in the twelve months pr"."oing the San Ramon valley
Regional Planning commission's decision o-n an upp"ur 
"onceming the tree permit, and
in the twelve months preceding the Board of Superv^isor's iecision on an appeal of a
commission decision on the tree permit, or on appeal of a plarming Department decision
on modification of the Final Development Plan. -ih" 
co.n*issioner is not an architect or
engineer and will not be preparing pion, o, drawings of a technical nature for the
homeowner.
ANALYSIS
The Act's conflict-of-interest provisions are to ensure that public officials will
perform their duties in an impartial manner, free from bias caused by their financial
interests' (Section 81001(b).) specifically, section 87ioo irorriuits any public official
from "making," "participating in making," or otherwise using his or trer oliciat position
to "influence" a govemmental decision in which the official has a financial interest.
The commi::,:l adopted an eight-step standard anarysis to decide whether an
official has a disqualifying conflict of i*nterest. (Regulation rg700(bx1)_(g).) The


File No. 4-08-029
Page No. 5
general ntle, however, is that a conflict of interest exists whenever a public official makes
a governmental decision that has a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on one
or more of his or her financial interests. (Section g7103.)
You have not requested advice as to whether the commissioner has a conflict of
interest with regard to decisions involving commissioner Bowlby,s client, the
homeowner whose property is the subject-of the ,r"" p".-i, upp.ul, *a ,pp.r of the
approval of the Mitigated Negative Declaration before the planning commission. Mr.
Bowlby acknowledges (1) that he is a public official (2) with a conflict of interest
prohibiting him, from making or partiCipating in making, any governmental decision rhat
will have a reasonably foreseeable material financial erie.t upon any source of income or
promised income to him, aggregating $500 or more durin gthe l2months preceding the
date of a particular decision of the pLnning commission. (Section g7r03(c); Reguration
18703.3.)
Rather, your question is limited to two aspects of Step 2 of the anarysis:
1. Influencing
There are two rules used to determine whether a public official is using or
attempting to use his or-her official position to influence a governmental decision. The
first rule applies when the relevant governmental decision ii within or before the public
official's own agency or an agency appointed by or subject to the budgetary control of
the public official's agency. (Regulaiion nloi.S(a).1 i;nder tfri, *r"] "iiffi, ,o
influence a decision include u publi" official's upp"u.u*.r or contacts before his or her
own agency, including any member, officer, .rpioy"" or consultant of that ag;; ;;'
behalf of a client.2
The second rule potentially applies when the relevant governmental decision is
within or before an agency other than: 1) the public official's i*, ug"n. y, i-i1 on
agency appointed by or subject to the budgetiry control of the publil orri"iut,, ugency.
(Regulation I 8702.3(b). )
which of these.two rules apply, depends upon the facts. while the commission
will generally treat each public body, evenwithin a single city or "ourry, u. 6urur"
public agencies, in some contexts a city or a county itself is considered a single agency,
with respect to Regulation rg702.3. (i{ons Advice Letter, No. I-00-25 5: LarmoreAdvice
Letter, No. 4-00-275: Stout Advice Lerter, No. I_gg_313.)
Commissioner Bowlby wishes to know if he may appear before or otherwise
communicate with the Board and shared staff of tne noara and planning .o**ir.io, on
behalf of the homeowner, his private client. You indicate that Contru Cortu county,s
ordinance, 26-2.202 establishes a single,.planning ug.r.y';-that ..consists of the
2 Commission staff hopes to examine this regulation in the near f'uture to determine whether
subdivision (a) of the regulation should be clarifred.


File No. A-08-029
Page No. 6
following divisions Board of Supervisors; Planning Commission; planning Department;
B oard of Appeals ; Zoning Administraror; ana Area ilanning Commi ss ions j,
In light of these facts, the factual scenario you describe in your question pertains
to the first mle because these county departments and their staffs are part of a single
"planning agency." Accordingly, commissioner Bowlby, who is un ur.o planning
commissioner, may not appear or communicate with -"*b.r, of the Board of
Supervisors or planning staff.
2. The exceptionfor representing an fficial,s ,,personar interests:,,
Regulation 18702.5 requires that a public official enumerated in Section g72OO
(including planning commissioners) who has a conflict of interest in a decision noticed at
a public meeting, must: (1) immediately prior to the discnssion of the item, orally identify
each type of economic interest involved in the decision as well as details of the economic
interest, as discussed in Regulation rg702.5(bxlxB), on the record of the meeting; (2)
recuse himself or herself; and (3) leave the roo* foi the duration of the discussion and/or
vote on the item.3 since Mr. Bowlby is a planning commi;;;;;,;;r,u"";rr-erared
in Section 87200, these requirementi apply to hini Therefore, he must leave the room
and cannot remain in the audience during ihe commission's consideration of the matter,
unless the exception in 18702.5(dx3) appties ro his situation.
You ask whether despite his conflict of interest, Commissioner Bowlby may
nonetheless appear.before the Planning commission to comment on the tree permit
appeal and before the Board of Supervisors to comment on possible appeals or tn"
Planning Commission's decision on the tree permit and modification of the Final
Development Plan. You state the commissioner wishes to appear before the planning
Commission and the Board as a resident of the Planned Unif bevelopment oirtri"t in the
same way and from the same location where members of the public can comment.
Regulation 18702.4 states that even if a conflict of interest is present, a public
official may appear before.his or her agency as any other member of the general public in
the course of its prescribed govemmental function in order to represent himself or herself
on matters related solely to his or her "personal interests." Such an appearance, properly
made, does not constitute,making, participating in making, or influen"ing a governmental
decision. (Regutation tB7 02.46)(i), (U)d ).)
An official's "personal interests" include, but are not limited to, an interest in real
property that is wholly owned by the official or members of his or her immediate family.
(Regulation 18702.4(b)(lXA).) For purposes of this analysis, we assume rhat
Commissioner Bowlby wholly o*rt th"..al property in tlhe planned unit Development
District.
I Forclosed sessions, consentcalendars. absences and speaking as a memberofthe public
regarding personal interesrs, special rules found in regulation fSiOiS ilf and (d) apply. (Section 87105).


File No. 4-08-029
Page No. 7
Because this regulation provides an exception to the rule prohibiting
Commissioner Bowlby from otherwise appearing before his agency to influence a
decision with which he has a conflict of iruerest,l is consrru.i ,o."*r/. (s;; oclerman
Advice Letter, 4-00-082; Gallogher Advice Letter No. r-94-279,. and TorranceAdvice
Letter, A-94-084.)
You have provided no facts that Commissioner Bowlby's residence, which is
2,900 feet -or more than half a mile away from the affected property _ would be
impacted by the tree permit. The only factor supporting the planning commissioner,s
claim of a personal interest is that other homeo*'n.., initre planned Unit Development
District may also want to expand their homes. The expansion of other homes is the
subject of different government decisions than the one at issue here. The decision in
question will not cause any other homeowner to expand his or her home. Thus, this
speculative "concem" would not trigger this narrow exception to the conflict of interest
ltll.. Accordingly, we conclude based on these facts thai the exception under Regulation
r8702.4(b) does not apply ro Commissioner Bowlby regarding porribt. uppJ, of the
tree permit and modification of the Finar Development Flan.
lf you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (g16) 322-5660.

You have provided no facts that Commissioner Bowlby's residence, which is

QUESTION: l) If the planning commissioner recuses himself on rhe record at the San Ramon
valley Regional Planning Commission meeting regarding the decision involving the tree
permit appeal, and he leaves the dias:
A) May he remain in the audience during the Commission's consideration of the
matters?

B) May he make public comment on these matters as a resident of the planned
Unit Development District in the same way and from the same location where members
of the public can comment?
2) While the tree permit appeal is pending before the San Ramon Valley Regional
Planning Commission, can the Commissioner, aCting as a representative of the
homeowner, communicate with members of the goaid of Supervisors about the appeal?
3) Once the decision of the San Ramon Valley Regional Planning Commission on
the tree permit appeal is final, if the matter is then appealed to the noard-of Supervisors:
A) May the commissioner, acting as a representative of the homeowner,
communicate with planning Department staff about the appeal?
B) May the commissioner, acting as a representative of the homeowner
communicate with members of the Board of Supervisors about the appeal?
C) During public hearings before the Board, may the commissioner appear on the
homeowner's behalf?
D) During public hearings before the Board, may the commissioner comment on
the appeal as a member of the public who lives in the planned unit development
district?
4) If the Planning Department's decision on modification of the Final
Development Plan is appealed to the Board of Supervisors:
A) May the Commissioner, acting as a representative of the homeowner
communicate with Planning Department staff about the appeal?
B) May the Commissioner, acting as a representative of the homeowner,
communicate with members of the Board of Supervisors about the appeal?
C) During public hearings before the Board, may the Commissioner appear on the
homeowner's behalf?
- 
D) During public hearings before the Board, may the Commissioner comment on
the appeal as a member of the public who lives in the pianned unit development district?

CONCLUSION: 1. Due to Commissioner Bowlby's conflict of interest in the tree permit decision
1! Uecause of his position as planning commissioner (a position enumerated in Section
87200), Mr. Bowlby must recuse himself on the record ai the planning commission
meeting and leave the room for the duration of the discussion and,/or vote on the item. He
cannot remain in the audience and may not comment during the commission,s
consideration of the matter because based on the facts provlded the exception in
18702.5(d)(3) does not apply in this situation.
2-4: Based on the facts you have presented, decisions and appeals before the San
lamon Valley Regional Planning Commission and the Board of Suplrvisors are
decisions before Commissioner Bowlby's own agency. Therefore, he is subject to the
broad prohibition which prevents him from communicating with any member, officer,
employee or consultant with his agency, which, accordingio your facts, is composed of
the various divisions ollh: counry's planning agency uniit, staff, including pranning
department staff. In addition, you donot pr"r.rt sufficient facts to support?hat the
Commissioner's personal interest is affecGd by the above decisions. i'he.efo.e, *e
conclude that he does not qualify for the exception under Regulation 1g702.4(b)and may
not comment on the appeals.
